title: feat(video): start+end frame via positional image[] across providers
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary

- Uses a shared positional video image contract: `image[0]` = start frame, `image[1]` = end frame; one image remains normal I2V.
- Adds end-frame support for `seedance`, `seedance-2.0`, `veo`, and `wan-fast`; unsupported video models continue to ignore `image[1]`.
- Makes `seedance-2.0` reject more than two images with HTTP 400 and sends fetched image bytes as data URIs to Replicate.
- Routes `wan-fast` start+end requests through Wan 2.2 KF2V, removes `audio_output` from its registry capabilities, and keeps rich capability metadata on `/models` and `/image/models`.
- Updates docs, schemas, SDK types, MCP model listing, and CLI model capability display for `video_capabilities`.

## Validation

- GitHub checks: `Biome Check` ✅, `CodeQL` ✅
- Local: `gen.pollinations.ai` typecheck ✅
- Local: `packages/sdk` typecheck ✅
- Local: `packages/polli-cli` build ✅
- Local: focused video/model vitest coverage ✅
- Staging: `seedance-2.0` with three images returns the new 400 ✅
- Staging: `/image/models` reports `wan-fast` as `start_frame,end_frame` only ✅
- Staging: start+end video generation works for `veo`, `seedance`, `seedance-2.0`, and `wan-fast` ✅

## Notes

`/v1/models` remains the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Rich model metadata, including `video_capabilities`, is exposed through `/models` and `/image/models`.

gist.headline: Connecting the frames
gist.blurb: You can now pass two images to our video models to define both the start and end frames. Perfect for creating smooth transitions, morphs, or forcing a specific final state.
gist.summary: Added support for start and end frames in video generation via a positional image[] array (image[0] for start, image[1] for end) across the seedance, seedance-2.0, veo, and wan-fast models.
gist.impact: Developers can now generate video transitions between two specific images by passing an array of URLs, with full support rolled out to the API, SDK, CLI, and MCP tools.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee acting as a movie director. Key symbols: film strips, two distinct picture frames or polaroids representing the start and end states. The robot companion helping hold the frames. The metaphor is connecting point A to point B.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny director's beret, holding a clapperboard next to a glowing film strip that connects two polaroid pictures. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holds one of the polaroids, its screen showing a happy face. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny director's beret, holding a clapperboard next to a glowing film strip that connects two polaroid pictures. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holds one of the polaroids, its screen showing a happy face. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.
